# services.soju

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.soju`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.soju.acceptProxyIP` | `list of string` | Allow the specified IPs to act as a proxy. Proxys have the ability to overwrite the remote and local connection addresses (via the X-Forwarded-\* HTTP header fields). The special name "localhost" accepts the loopback addresses 127.0.0.0/8 and ::1/128. By default, all IPs are rejected. |
| `services.soju.adminSocket.enable` | `boolean` | Listen for admin connections from sojuctl at /run/soju/admin. |
| `services.soju.configFile` | `absolute path` | Path to config file. If this option is set, it will override any configuration done using other options, including {option}`extraConfig`. |
| `services.soju.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable soju. |
| `services.soju.enableMessageLogging` | `boolean` | Whether to enable message logging. |
| `services.soju.extraConfig` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Lines added verbatim to the generated configuration file. |
| `services.soju.hostName` | `string` | Server hostname. |
| `services.soju.httpOrigins` | `list of string` | List of allowed HTTP origins for WebSocket listeners. The parameters are interpreted as shell patterns, see {manpage}`glob(7)`. |
| `services.soju.listen` | `list of string` | Where soju should listen for incoming connections. See the `listen` directive in {manpage}`soju(1)`. |
| `services.soju.package` | `package` | The soju package to use. |
| `services.soju.tlsCertificate` | `null or absolute path` | Path to server TLS certificate. |
| `services.soju.tlsCertificateKey` | `null or absolute path` | Path to server TLS certificate key. |
